heading: Self-Guided Migration Tools
body:
- To load data into a new cluster in Atlas, see [Load Sample Data Into Atlas](sample-data).

- To make a copy of your cluster for testing purposes, see [MongoDB Backup Methods](manual/core/backups).

- To load data from a JSON or a CSV file into an Atlas cluster, use the [Compass](https://www.mongodb.com/try/download/compass) GUI (Graphical User Interface) .

- To create backups and restore data, use the MongoCLI utilities [mongorestore and mongodump](manual/tutorial/backup-and-restore-tools).

- To migrate from legacy relational databases to MongoDB, use the [Relational Migrator](relational-migrator) tool.
  Relational Migrator utilizes intelligent algorithms and generative AI to automate solutions to common data modeling, code conversion, and other migration challenges.

- To restore data from an Atlas cluster backup to another Atlas cluster, use [download those snapshots](restore-overview).

- If the application that you want to migrate requires a near-continuous uptime, contact [MongoDB Support](request-support)
  and share your uptime requirements and cluster configuration.
